title: We are one of the biggest spot copy traders in Binance: AMA
author: ethogos
selftext:
Hi all, Ethogos team here,

You can find our spot public trading portfolio here:

https://www.binance.com/en/copy-trading/lead-details/4369905518238103296?timeRange=180D

(Sharing full link so you can see it’s from the oficial Binance page, you can also look us there)

A little bit about the team: Ethogos started almost 6 years ago (as a group of friends with economics, maths & physics backgrounds) who were convinced that the cryptocurrency markets were inefficient and if we successfully identified these inefficiencies we could profit from them.

After one year of testing we decided to go live: it went catastrophically wrong due to our lack of experience, we almost blew the account and had to rethink our approach. Since then we have averaged almost 60% yearly (just shy of 4% monthly) with a Sharpe ratio of over 3 for 180 days over most of this period.

Here are our results over the last 6 months: a little over 67.19% ROI (around 60% net of fees). We trade mostly BTC now as we have grown to a portfolio that is substantially bigger than before, so trading altcoins basically causes a movement in the price more often than not and causes us to lose some alpha.

Finally we have helped our copiers make almost 80k net of fees, which for us has been a great honor and responsibility. We work closely with our biggest investors to advise on asset allocation – we do not want anyone to go all in on this. As you can see the maximum drawdown was 8.32%, including the flash crash which when compared to a 60% ROI is… well, impressive. Finally our win rate of 87% means that we nail more than 5 out of 6 trades – again an amazing stat.

We wanted to post this thread because:
	1.	It has been hard to get new followers/interactions.
	2.	We believe it will help many people who are stuck investing with bad copy traders get better results.
	3.	Clarifying the metrics you should take into account will also make you understand better the risks of whoever you are copying.
	4.	A lot of people copy us the wrong way (fixed amount instead of fixed ratio) which basically makes it so that the strategy is less profitable.

We have allocated 100 USDT as prizes for the Top 3 most upvoted answers. They can either be questions to us, the strategy we use, our experience, approach etc - upvoted comments not related to trading are not eligible for prizes.

comments.author: medster87
comments.body: Whats the minimum recommended amount to go in for copying ?

replies.author: ethogos
replies.body: I don’t think there is a minimum amount to go in for copying. What I would do is not assign more than 20% to our public copy portfolio mainly due to the volatility it has

comments.author: moory52
comments.body:
I have been away from Crypto for almost 2 years and came back a week before the recent crash. It did not help at all. Very interesting results. Few questions:

1- Why only spot? Is the strategy not fit for futures? (I only trade spots but just wondering)
2- Could we have some insights about the strategy and what didn’t work before changing your approach?
3- Is there a ceiling to how much capital i can use for copy trading this bot on Binance?

replies.author: ethogos
replies.body:
1. We only do spot because our strategy was originally developed only using spot data. When we tried to take it to the futures market the fluctuations in funding fees, the effects of leverage (even small) and other factors make it so it does not work so well. We are currently testing a new futures strategy (on our public futures portfolio) but it’s on beta and we wouldn’t advice anyone to trade on it.

2. Clearly we won’t reveal the full strategy but we have two main components: self-developed sentiment indexes and DeFi valuation mean reversals. Briefly explaining each:
We use a self-developed sentiment index because if you use publicly and easily available information you are “seeing” and using what the rest does, you will not stand out.
And by DeFi valuation mean reversals are basically the valuation of DeFi proyects as if they were a stock company. We take into account fees, revenue, buyback, total proyect value etc. The main idea is that when valuations spike (are too high) when compared to the EMA o MA a reversal is likely to happen, which in our experience also correlates to a decrease in BTC’s and thus other altcoins prices.

3. I don’t think there is a ceiling, we do have been encountering an issue I must admit that as we raise capital our “edge” or alpha diminishes because we now start being market makers (sometimes when buying low market cap - about 200-300M -  proyects we might move the price by 1-2%, which reduces our uplift). That is one of the reasons why we are moving more into trading BTC and we use our personal funds for this small opportunities.

comments.author: Metni_erni
comments.body:
Hi Ethogos team. I've been closely analyzing your monthly performance reports and noticed a fascinating pattern:
​Your strategy (especially SPOT CT) seems to generate significant positive alpha when the market is down or choppy (e.g., your +7.96% in August when BTC was -6.49%).
​However, it appears to lag or even post a loss during strong, linear bull market months (e.g., your -2.65% in July when BTC was +8.04%).
​My Question: Is this performance profile a deliberate design choice? In other words, is your model fundamentally built to be a 'market-neutral' or 'noise-filtering' strategy that profits from volatility/downtrends, rather than a 'trend-following' strategy designed to ride bull runs?"

replies.author: ethogos
replies.body:
Hi Metni!
That’s a great insight. As mentioned in other comments, we trade based on sentiment and mean reversion in valuations. The implication is that when BTC’s price increases, project valuations typically skyrocket. By that time, we’re usually fully out of our positions or gradually selling as valuations rise relative to intrinsic value.

Afterward, we look for a new reversal — opportunities to buy back into “cheap” valuations once the market corrects. When this happens, there’s often a crash, and we profit by buying at those lower levels. If the price keeps rising without a correction, we usually don’t have a confirmed entry. So we actually rely on BTC price drops before re-entering the market.
